summary: The test suite thoroughly covers every stated requirement: cartesian product generation, include/exclude rules (including partial-key excludes and merge-vs-standalone include behaviour), max-parallel, fail-fast, size validation, JSON output, and an end-to-end integration scenario. Organisation is excellent — Context blocks map cleanly to feature areas, test names are descriptive, each test is self-contained, and the end-to-end scenario includes inline arithmetic comments that double as living documentation. Rigor is strong but not exhaustive: boundary conditions (exact MaxSize, fail-fast explicitly false, zero max-parallel omission) are well covered, yet a few gaps exist — the code path that throws for an empty dimension array is never exercised, there are no tests for duplicate values within a dimension, and no test verifies that the JSON keys are sorted deterministically. The include-merging tests also only cover a single matching combo; merging across multiple matches is untested. These are minor omissions in an otherwise high-quality suite that would reliably catch regressions in all core behaviour.
